title: Story Acceptance Criteria as First-Class Table with verification_criterion Field

story.as_a: tenant operator trusting that story verification re-executes the implementer's work rather than accepting their self-report
story.i_want_to: have each acceptance criterion on a story promoted from jsonb-on-story to a first-class table row, with a machine-checkable verification_criterion describing what code artifact proves it's implemented
story.so_that: loopctl's verification engine can independently re-check each AC against the actual committed code instead of trusting the implementer

rationale: Lazy-bastard defense (section 3 L3 of chain-of-custody-v2.md) requires independent re-execution. That requires knowing what to check. The existing ACs are jsonb blobs with human-readable descriptions — unverifiable programmatically. Promoting to a first-class table with structured bindings lets the verification runner (US-26.4.2) actually check each one.

acceptance_criteria.description: Migration creates `story_acceptance_criteria` table: id (uuid PK), tenant_id (FK), story_id (FK), ac_id (string, like 'AC-26.1.1'), description (text), verification_criterion (jsonb), status (enum: pending, verified, failed, unverifiable), verified_at (timestamptz nullable), verified_by_dispatch_id (FK nullable), evidence_path (text nullable), inserted_at, updated_at.

`verification_criterion` jsonb accepts one of 5 type tags: `{type: "test", path, test_name}`, `{type: "code", path, line_range, pattern}`, `{type: "route", method, path}`, `{type: "migration", table, column, column_type}`, `{type: "manual", description}`. A check constraint or application-level validator ensures the jsonb conforms to one of the shapes.

Backfill migration: for every existing story with jsonb-blob ACs, extract each AC into a row in the new table. Default `verification_criterion` to `{type: "manual", description: "legacy"}` for backfilled rows. Legacy rows are flagged and require operator approval to verify per US-26.4.4.

`Loopctl.WorkBreakdown.contract_story/3` validates that every AC in the contract has a `verification_criterion` OR explicitly uses `{type: "manual", description: ...}`. Stories with legacy 'manual: legacy' criteria get a warning but are not rejected.
`GET /api/v1/stories/:id/acceptance_criteria` returns the full AC list with verification status. Tenant-scoped.
`list_stories` and `get_story` responses include the new AC list (replacing the jsonb blob in story.metadata).
The story-writer skill in claude-config is updated to emit the new verification_criterion shape in every AC it generates (documented in mcp-server README).
Tenant isolation: AC queries enforce tenant_id. Cross-tenant access returns 404.
OpenAPI spec updates: new `AcceptanceCriterion` schema, new operation specs. CHANGELOG entry. System article stub `acceptance-criteria-bindings` reserved for US-26.4.5.

test_cases.name: Contract story with test-type criterion
steps: POST /stories with ACs containing verification_criterion of type test
expected_results: story_acceptance_criteria has a row per AC with the criterion stored

test_cases.name: Invalid criterion type rejected at contract time
Backfill creates manual:legacy rows for existing stories
preconditions: Existing story with jsonb AC blob
expected_results: story_acceptance_criteria has one row per AC

technical_notes: Check constraint on verification_criterion shape is verbose in SQL — prefer application-level validation via Ecto.Changeset.
The story-writer skill update is a sibling PR in claude-config, linked from this story.
